summary: Task 1 refactor is clean, idiomatic Kotlin and matches Decision 5 of the tech-spec exactly. All three log levels (e/i/w) collapse to thin wrappers around a single private write(level, ...) helper that owns whitelist enforcement, sanitize, length-bounding, mutex, append, and rotation — zero duplication, zero drift risk. e() public signature is unchanged, i()/w() mirror it with the same cause: Throwable? = null default. ALLOWED_COMPONENTS grows 14 -> 15 with "inference-reset". KDoc is updated to describe the three levels and the shared pipeline. :core-runtime stays UI-free (verified by grep). Pre-existing patterns (mutex+IO ordering, require-before-I/O, swallow-on-write-failure with explicit comment) are preserved. No critical or major findings; one minor nit on documenting the Level.name -> printed-prefix contract.

findings.issue: buildLine relies on the implicit contract that Level.name (the Kotlin enum constant identifier) equals the printed prefix word. This is true today (ERROR / INFO / WARN), but a future rename of the enum constant — e.g. for stylistic reasons — would silently change the on-disk log format, which is a public contract pinned by tests in inferenceResetComponent_acceptedByAllLevels and consumed by the future Diagnostics screen (AC-1.5).
findings.fix: Either (a) add a one-line KDoc on the private enum class Level { ERROR, INFO, WARN } noting that constant names are the literal log prefix and must not be renamed, or (b) give Level an explicit val prefix: String field and reference level.prefix in buildLine. (a) is the minimum-diff option and consistent with the spec's hint to use level.name.

findings.issue: The catch (_: Throwable) {} block silently swallows I/O failures inside write(). The behaviour is intentional and correctly justified by the inline comment ("Logger must never fail its caller"), and CancellationException is properly re-thrown one line above — so this is not a regression and is consistent with the Phase 1 baseline. Flagging only for visibility because the code-reviewing checklist treats catch-swallow as auto-major; here the explicit comment + CE-rethrow + log-of-last-resort semantics make it the correct trade-off.
findings.fix: No action required. Optionally, narrow the catch to IOException to make the intent typed (would still need to keep CancellationException re-throw), but this changes behaviour for non-IO Throwables and is out of scope for a mechanical refactor.

ac_check.ac: shared write() helper — no duplication of whitelist / sanitize / truncation / mutex / rotation
ac_check.evidence: ErrorLog.kt:84-94 — e()/i()/w() are 3-line wrappers each delegating to write(). ErrorLog.kt:96-124 — write() is the single owner of require(component in ALLOWED_COMPONENTS), mutex.withLock, withContext(Dispatchers.IO), buildLine (which calls sanitize+truncate via take(maxLen)), file.appendText, and the >MAX_LOG_BYTES rotation block. No copy of those operations exists in e/i/w.

ac_check.ac: e() remains public with same signature suspend fun e(component: String, description: String, cause: Throwable? = null)
ac_check.evidence: ErrorLog.kt:84 declares exactly that signature. Public visibility (no internal/private modifier). Backward-compatible for existing Phase 1/2/3 callers (download, inference-init, inference, inference-cleanup, settings-io, camera, audio, attachment-decode, model, engine-warmup, history-read, history-write, attachment-save, attachment-read).

ac_check.ac: i() and w() have cause: Throwable? = null default and same shape as e()
ac_check.evidence: ErrorLog.kt:88 — suspend fun i(component: String, description: String, cause: Throwable? = null). ErrorLog.kt:92 — suspend fun w(component: String, description: String, cause: Throwable? = null). Identical parameter list, default value, and suspend modifier as e().

ac_check.ac: Format prefix is 'ERROR '/'INFO '/'WARN ' exactly (one trailing space)
ac_check.evidence: ErrorLog.kt:132 — head = "${level.name} [$component] ...". The single space sits between level.name and the literal '['. Tests pin this: ErrorLogTest.kt:86-88 assert startsWith("ERROR [inference-reset] ") / "INFO [inference-reset] " / "WARN [inference-reset] ". Pre-existing tests (descriptionSanitization_*, descriptionTruncation_*, causeChainBounding_*, nullCauseMessage_*) still anchor the ERROR-level format string to "ERROR [inference] ..." verbatim, confirming no drift on the legacy path.

ErrorLog.kt:33-53 — the Set<String> literal contains 14 prior entries (download, inference-init, inference, inference-cleanup, settings-io, camera, audio, attachment-decode, model, engine-warmup, history-read, history-write, attachment-save, attachment-read) plus the new 'inference-reset' under a '// Phase 3.6' comment marker (line 51-52). Total = 15. ErrorLogTest.kt:72-75 explicitly asserts ALLOWED_COMPONENTS.size == 15 and 'inference-reset' in ALLOWED_COMPONENTS.

ac_check.ac: KDoc updated to mention the three levels and shared bounding/rotation
ac_check.evidence: ErrorLog.kt:55-78 — class-level KDoc rewritten: opens with 'On-device three-level writer (`e` / `i` / `w`)', explicitly states 'All three route through one private [write] helper that owns whitelist enforcement, sanitization, length-bounding, mutex, append, and rotation — so adding a level cannot drift from the existing input pipeline', shows the new <LEVEL> [component] ... format template, and clarifies that <LEVEL> is one of ERROR/INFO/WARN. The patterns.md cross-reference is preserved at line 77. The whitelist KDoc (lines 23-32) was also updated to reference [ErrorLog.e] / [ErrorLog.i] / [ErrorLog.w] and call out 'before any I/O'.

ac_check.ac: :core-runtime remains UI-free (no androidx.compose|androidx.activity imports in src/main)
ac_check.evidence: Grep on path core-runtime/src/main with pattern 'androidx\.(compose|activity)' returned no matches. The diff for ErrorLog.kt adds zero new imports — only existing android.content.Context, dagger.hilt qualifiers, javax.inject, and kotlinx.coroutines remain. The new private enum class Level is self-contained.

ac_check.ac: No regressions in pre-existing ErrorLog behaviour (cause formatting, null-cause tail, sanitize/truncate semantics, mutex+IO ordering, rotation, whitelist-rejection-before-file-open)
ac_check.evidence: Mutex acquired before withContext(Dispatchers.IO) — same as pre-refactor (write at lines 105-106). The require() check stays outside the mutex/IO blocks (line 102-104) so IAE still throws before any file open — verified by retained tests unknownComponent_doesNotWriteFile (ErrorLogTest.kt:174-181) and the new unknownComponent_stillRejected_negative (ErrorLogTest.kt:92-118) which asserts assertFalse(logFile.exists()) for all three levels. CancellationException propagation (line 117-118) and the documented swallow-other-Throwables policy (line 119-121) are preserved verbatim. Rotation block (lines 112-116) is unchanged. buildLine still emits ' :: CauseType: causeMsg' only when cause != null and uses cause::class.simpleName.orEmpty() + sanitize(message, 200) — pre-existing nullCauseMessage_emitsEmptyTail and causeChainBounding_* tests still anchor that path.
